selected_idea.title: 0.01 Degree
selected_idea.one_liner: Eco-terrorists send a nanomachine 67 million years back to save the dinosaurs, but a saboteur programmer reverses their 0.01-degree asteroid deflection, preserving human history.
selected_idea.anti_trope: The hero wins through a single line of code, not combat or superpowers
hard_constraints: Time travel is one-way — no communication with the past
Nanomachine operates autonomously for ~1 million years
0.01 degree precision limit on asteroid deflection
Peter can only act through code, not physical confrontation

constraints_inherited.anti_trope: Hero saves humanity not through action or power, but through quiet intellectual sabotage — one conditional bug in navigation code
hard_constraints: No supernatural elements — hard sci-fi only
Peter cannot physically fight the antagonists
The satellite cannot verify its trajectory while in the asteroid's shadow
Once sent through the black hole, the nanomachine is beyond anyone's control
constraints_inherited.protagonist_flaw: Passive, demoralized, displaced by AI — an unlikely and reluctant hero
constraints_inherited.antagonist_mirror: Both sides want to fix a broken world; they differ on whether humanity deserves to exist
constraints_inherited.opening_hook: A cosmic fragment drifts toward Earth — 67 million years later, the same asteroid's fate depends on 0.01 degrees
